text: Our efforts have attracted external attention, and we are seen as one of the leading players in our industry. During the year we were ranked number one of 200 companies in Fashion Revolution’s What Fuels Fashion report, topped Stand.earth’s Fossil-Free Fashion Scorecard for the second consecutive year and were A-listed by CDP for our climate and water efforts. What we are particularly proud of is that many of the DESIGNED AROUND effects we have achieved have been driven by smarter THE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE ways of working in our value chain, rather than by large “In 2025 we investments. We work with fewer suppliers and build Inspiration long-term partnerships with them. By offering more stable laid a stronger volumes and better planning, we create the conditions and presence for our partners to invest in renewable energy and more foundation and energy-efficient processes. It is this partnership that took important makes a difference. When business and sustainability in all channels develop together, we can reduce emissions at scale steps towards and show that fashion can be both affordable and have a lower climate impact. all our long-term The power of our culture t argets.” Our purpose is Liberating fashion for the many Our greatest assets are our employees and our shared – and in all our channels this means creating expevalues. The passion I encounter in stores, warehouses and riences that inspire and engage in a way that feels offices around the world – the desire to succeed and the relevant and meaningful to our customers. Our drive to always improve – is one of our most fundamental products are always the starting point.
notes: Mix: CDP A-list + Stand.earth (credible) vs 'Liberating fashion' brand language (vague).

text: INDITEX GENERAL E. ENVIRONMENTAL S. SOCIAL G. GOVERNANCE ANNEXES INDEPENDENT AT A GLANCE INFORMATION INFORMATION INFORMATION INFORMATION VERIFICATION REPORT Transition risks E1.IRO-1_10; E1.IRO-1_11; E1.IRO-1_12; E1.SBM-3_01; E1.SBM-3_06 Transition risks vary significantly depending on the level of ambition of each pathway analysed, and they may affect different areas of our business. To gauge their potential impact, we have assessed various dimensions of transition risks in the short, medium and long term: Regulatory, Legal liability, Technological, Market and Reputation. /Transition risk: envisages establishing an explicit carbon price as a possible measure for governments to incentivise the transition to a low-carbon economy. /Legal liability: considers developments in connection with climaterelated litigation. /Technological: analyses how the development and use of new technologies that are less GHG-intensive would affect the Group. /Market: envisages an increasing market interest in sustainable products and services. /Reputation: considers a context with no progress towards a lowcarbon economy, valuing the effects of public opinion, climate activism and investors’ perceptions. The level of uncertainty associated with analysing the impacts of climate change is particularly significant in the case of transition risk, because the underlying assumptions of each scenario are based on projections made at a given point in time about how future events may evolve, such as legislation in different countries, technological innovations, consumer preferences, and the extent of litigation on sustainability issues, for example. These assumptions are inherently volatile, as they depend on external developments. Furthermore, there is often insufficiently comprehensive or robust historical data available for application to the textile sector.
notes: Risk assessment narrative — describes uncertainty rather than making a claim.

text: Registered number 17 July 2025 04162033 57 PRINCIPLE: NAKED PRODUCTS SINCE 2005 Potentially saved 400+ million bottles from becoming waste Equivalent to 6 hours of global plastic bottle production Registered number 17 July 2025 04162033 58 PRINCIPLE: NAKED PRODUCTS Registered number 17 July 2025 04162033 59 Principle: Digital Ethics Evolving Principle: Lush will always endeavour to behave with an ethical conscience in our ever-evolving digital estate. We embrace open-source technologies in everything that we design, build and release. We give back our research and code to open communities. We commit to using conflict material free commodity hardware that has high output but a low energy consumption powered by renewable green energy across the group, where possible. We ensure that all of Lush’s staff and customer data is encrypted, secure and transparent. Our Customers and Staff have the right to know what information we hold about them. Registered number 17 July 2025 04162033 60
notes: 'Where possible' weakens energy claim; 400M bottle figure is an estimate; mixed with digital ethics.

text: PRINCIPLE: DIGITAL ETHICS Lush Digital believes technology should give more than it takes from society and the environment. We champion ethical hardware, ethical data, ethical design, and open source technologies and we support and elevate communities who feel the same. Just as Lush believes in ethically sourced ingredients, our Digital business believes in ethical tech that gives back to the community. To keep us accountable to the beliefs that we hold true as a technological business, Lush Digital formulated its own set of Digital Ethics Policies to consider every time we design, build or launch a digital product. They can be consolidated into three categories: Open Source, Ethical Hardware and Ethical Data. Our open source policy aims to equally contribute to open-source tech communities, wherever we can, in order to support the grassroots world of creating tech for everyone to share and benefit from without the heavy burden of cost. We are committed to the principles of open source and in that spirit, we strive to use technologies with OSI-approved licenses and to contribute our research and code back to open communities under such licenses. Our Ethical Hardware policy guides us to choose the best supplier we can in terms of their approach to sourcing materials, reparability, energy consumption and production methods used. Our Ethical Data policy is about ensuring that all of Lush’s staff and customer data is secure and transparent as our customers and staff have the right to know what we hold about them. It states that we will ensure all data that is stored on our products or systems is encrypted and secure, that we will ensure your data can be accessed by you only and those you give permission to and that we are transparent about how we use customer and staff data.
notes: Digital ethics policies — not environmental claims.

text: Co Create is an initiative that works to bring new minds, voices and cultures into the product creation process. People within the company are given the opportunity to come together and design product ranges that represent their cultural or religious celebrations, with the aim to connect to more customers. Co-Create have worked on many ranges since the project's inception 3 years ago, representing celebrations and holidays that include Eid al-Fitr, Eid al-Adha, Hanukkah, Día De Muertos, Diwali and St Patrick's Day, with more coming soon! We also recently launched our Gender Affirming Care At Work Policy which is live in the Netherlands, the UK and Ireland, with a hope to launch in more markets soon. This policy allows our trans and non binary colleagues peace of mind by providing a clear policy on work place support, including name and pronoun changes, transitioning leave and access to facilities. It was essential that we work with the LGBTQIA+ Community Network and the Trans community more specifically when creating this policy to ensure it truly reflected their lived experiences, needs and priorities. Registered number 17 July 2025 04162033 30 PRINCIPLE: ALL ARE WELCOME, ALWAYS Registered number 17 July 2025 04162033 31 Principle: Commitment to Transparency and External Audits Core Principle: Lush will contract regular third party audits of its Animal Testing Policy, its ethical and environmental practices as well as regular business audits. Registered number 17 July 2025 04162033 32
notes: Social/governance content (LGBTQIA+ policy) — not environmental.

text: Evolving Principles These are the values or principles we regard as so core to our philosophy that they will never be changed without proper consideration and consultation with all Lush Group Employees, so their views are taken into account when considering what action to take in relation to that proposed change. Registered number 17 July 2025 04162033 36 Principle: A Living Planet Evolving Principle: Lush believes it’s a part of the natural world, therefore its impact – be it through the ingredients we use, the way we manufacture and transport goods or how we generate energy – must give back more than it takes. Registered number 17 July 2025 04162033 37 PRINCIPLE: A LIVING PLANET Where we have the biggest impact on the living world is through our supply web. For our business to thrive - any business, really - we depend on clean water, healthy pollinators and abundant flora and fauna. Here is how we look at having a positive impact on nature. See the Renewable Energy, Environmental Policies and Recycled Materials Principles for more. Improving Farming by value are minimally processed natural materials, essential oils and absolutes. About half of that, our top Of all the raw natural materials, like cocoa and materials we buy, almonds, and essential oil plants like 62% lavender and rose, are estimated to take up 5,255 hectares of land.
notes: Aspirational philosophy mixed with land use data — genuinely uncertain.
